requirements.title: Synthetic Validation Draft Isolation
requirements.body: Supported internal validation or parity flows must not leave synthetic draft changes in live ADV project state. Protection must preserve legitimate user-created drafts and keep draft/status surfaces focused on real changes.

scenarios.title: Synthetic validation families blocked on supported create path
given: A supported internal validation or parity flow attempts to create a synthetic draft change matching a reserved parity-validation family on live ADV state
then: The synthetic draft is not persisted to the live project state
The caller receives a clear error or bounded degraded outcome directing synthetic activity to isolated temp/test storage

scenarios.title: Legitimate parity wording remains allowed
given: A normal user-driven change proposal uses benign wording that mentions parity but does not match a reserved synthetic family
then: The draft change is persisted normally
The protection does not block or rename the legitimate draft

scenarios.title: Draft and status surfaces stay clear after validation activity
given: Supported internal validation activity has run
scenarios.when: adv_change_list with status draft or adv_status is executed on the live project
then: Stale synthetic parity-validation drafts are absent from live draft results
Real user-authored drafts remain visible

requirements.title: Adversarial Review Enforcement
requirements.body: /adv-review and /adv-harden must enforce a minimum findings threshold to prevent shallow 'LGTM' behavior. /adv-review must run mandatory remediation that fixes all blocker/issue findings, investigates all suggestions/questions, implements validated suggestions, and runs cleanup before final verdict.

scenarios.title: Minimum findings validation
given: A review with fewer than 3 non-nit findings
then: The gate remains open and requires explicit justification for the clean result

scenarios.title: Review remediation is mandatory
given: A review produces blocker, issue, suggestion, or question findings
then: All blocker and issue findings are fixed and verified
Each suggestion/question is investigated and marked validated or rejected with evidence
Validated suggestions are implemented
A cleanup pass runs before final verdict is emitted

requirements.title: Status Config Diagnostics and Feature Flags
requirements.body: adv_status must surface project.json diagnostics and include parsed feature flag values so agents can see config health and runtime policy settings without opening files.

scenarios.title: Invalid project config is surfaced
given: project.json is malformed or schema-invalid
then: Output includes a config error or warning recommendation
The command does not fail hard due to config parse issues

scenarios.title: Feature flags are visible in status output
given: project.json parses successfully
then: Output includes feature_flags values
Defaults are applied when flags are omitted

requirements.title: Task Metadata Filter Semantics
requirements.body: Tasks may include optional metadata key/value pairs. adv_task_list must support has_metadata_key:<key> and metadata:<key>=<value> filters with behavior aligned between workflow-owned source-of-truth state and any derived query or index surface.

scenarios.title: Filter by metadata key
given: A change with tasks containing metadata keys
scenarios.when: adv_task_list is called with filter has_metadata_key:<key>
then: Only tasks containing that metadata key are returned

scenarios.title: Filter by metadata key/value
given: A change with tasks containing metadata key/value pairs
scenarios.when: adv_task_list is called with filter metadata:<key>=<value>
then: Only tasks matching both key and value are returned

requirements.title: Bounded Signal Flush on Shutdown
requirements.body: On SIGINT/SIGTERM, the plugin must run a bounded flush path before close, with idempotent/reentrant handling so duplicate signals cannot trigger multiple concurrent flush sequences.

scenarios.title: Signal performs bounded flush
given: The process receives SIGINT or SIGTERM
then: store.flush is attempted before store.close
A hard timeout bounds flush duration

scenarios.title: Duplicate signals are idempotent
given: A shutdown flush is already in progress
then: No second flush path starts
Shutdown remains deterministic

requirements.title: Durable Proposal Context for adv-task
requirements.body: After Quick Contract confirmation, /adv-task must always persist contract context to proposal.md, and downstream workflows must tolerate missing/empty legacy proposal files via scaffold fallback warnings.

scenarios.title: adv-task writes proposal by default
given: A Quick Contract is confirmed in /adv-task
then: proposal.md is written in the change directory
The file includes intent, scope, and success criteria

scenarios.title: Legacy missing proposal is non-blocking
given: A legacy change has missing or empty proposal.md
then: A scaffold proposal is generated
A non-blocking warning is emitted

requirements.title: Problem Statement Agreement for adv-proposal
requirements.body: /adv-proposal must extract prior discussion context (decisions, rejected approaches, constraints, open questions) from the conversation before synthesizing a problem statement, confirm it via the question tool before creating any change artifacts, and persist the confirmed text (including prior decisions and rejected approaches) as the opening section of proposal.md via the proposal parameter in adv_change_create. The problem statement must not contradict, omit, or reinterpret any prior decision or constraint from the conversation.

scenarios.title: Prior discussion context extracted before synthesis
given: A user invokes /adv-proposal after a conversation containing decisions, constraints, or rejected approaches
then: The agent extracts agreed facts, decisions made, rejected approaches, open questions, and constraints stated from the conversation
Empty categories are listed as 'None identified' rather than omitted
No decisions or constraints are fabricated that were not explicitly discussed

scenarios.title: Problem statement grounded in prior discussion
given: Prior discussion context has been extracted
then: The problem statement includes Prior Decisions, Rejected Approaches, and Open Questions sections
The problem statement does not contradict any extracted agreed fact
The problem statement does not reintroduce any rejected approach as a proposed solution
The problem statement does not ignore any stated constraint

scenarios.title: Drift detection in confirmation
given: A problem statement block is shown to the user
scenarios.when: The user reviews it via inline handoff text per docs/command-voice-standard.md § Inline Approval Voice (Tier A)
then: The presentation explicitly asks the user to check Prior Decisions and Rejected Approaches for accuracy
A 'Drift detected' reply path is documented for the user to flag discrepancies (free-form revise reply or explicit drift wording)
If drift is detected, the agent re-extracts and re-synthesizes before proceeding

scenarios.title: Confirmed problem statement persisted in proposal.md
given: The user confirms the problem statement in Phase 1
scenarios.when: The change is created in Phase 2
then: adv_change_create is called with the proposal parameter containing the confirmed text
proposal.md includes the confirmed problem statement as the Why section
proposal.md includes a Constraints from Discussion section with prior decisions and rejected approaches

scenarios.title: Abort path creates no artifacts
given: The user selects Abort during Phase 1 confirmation
then: No change.json is created
No proposal.md is created
No tasks are added

scenarios.title: Confirmed problem statement persisted as standalone artifact
given: The user confirms the problem statement in Phase 1
scenarios.when: The change is created in Phase 2
then: adv_change_create is called with the problemStatement parameter containing the confirmed problem statement text
A problem-statement.md file is written to the change directory as a sibling of proposal.md
The problem-statement.md content exactly matches the confirmed text (no template wrapping)
The tool output includes problemStatementPath pointing to the artifact
When the change is archived, problem-statement.md is preserved in the archive directory

requirements.title: Defensive and Nesting Slop Detection
requirements.body: /adv-slop-scan must detect overly defensive code (redundant guard chains, paranoid null checks, unreachable fallback branches) and deeply nested code (nesting depth >= configured threshold) using AST-first analysis with deterministic degraded fallback when AST tools are unavailable. Findings must include structured diagnostic fields in all output formats.

scenarios.title: Deep nesting detected via AST
given: A source file containing a function with nesting depth >= nesting_depth_threshold (default 4)
An AST analysis tool (ESLint, radon, or gocyclo) is available
then: A finding is emitted with smell ID MAINT-004
The finding includes nestingDepth, complexity, confidence, and detectionMethod fields
detectionMethod is 'ast'

scenarios.title: Defensive overkill detected
given: A source file containing a function with >= defensive_guard_threshold (default 3) redundant guard patterns on the same value
then: A finding is emitted with smell ID QUAL-011
The finding includes confidence and detectionMethod fields
Severity is at least medium

scenarios.title: Degraded fallback annotated when AST unavailable
given: No AST analysis tool is installed for the detected language
then: Nesting detection falls back to brace/indent counter
Findings from fallback include detectionMethod: 'degraded'
Report annotates affected findings with [DEGRADED: AST tool unavailable]

scenarios.title: Project threshold overrides respected
given: project.json contains features.slop_scan.nesting_depth_threshold: 6
then: Functions with nesting depth 4 or 5 are NOT flagged
Functions with nesting depth >= 6 ARE flagged

scenarios.title: Clean code produces no false positives
given: A source file with a single null check and a single try/catch block
then: No QUAL-011 or MAINT-004 findings are emitted for that file

requirements.title: Mid-Change Scope Expansion Re-Entry
requirements.body: When new objectives or acceptance criteria are introduced after a change has already progressed through the gate workflow, the added scope must be routed back through the earliest invalidated pre-implementation gate via adv_change_reenter before execution continues. Agents may trigger this re-entry autonomously; explicit user approval is not required to circle back to an earlier gate. Unaffected approved scope may continue without re-entry, and non-invalidating clarifications do not require re-entry.

scenarios.title: Scope expansion triggers re-entry
given: A change is in execution or a later gate
New objectives or acceptance criteria are discovered that were not part of the approved agreement
scenarios.when: adv_change_reenter is used from the earliest affected gate
then: The newly added scope is routed back through discovery, agreement, design, and planning as needed before execution resumes
Execution does not silently absorb the new scope without re-entry

scenarios.title: Unaffected approved scope continues without re-entry
given: A change has approved scope already in execution
A newly discovered item does not invalidate the existing approved work
then: Previously approved scope may continue without reopening unrelated gates
Only the newly invalidated scope is routed back through re-entry

scenarios.title: Non-invalidating clarification does not require re-entry
given: A change is in progress
A clarification is discovered that does not alter objectives, acceptance criteria, or design intent
then: adv_change_reenter is not required
Execution may continue within the existing scope

scenarios.title: Re-entry may proceed without explicit user approval
given: A change needs scope-expansion re-entry
then: The call may succeed without approvedByUser or approvalEvidence
approvalEvidence is optional audit context when re-entry follows an explicit user instruction

requirements.title: Re-Entry Cascade Reset Preserves Work
requirements.body: Reopening a change from gate X must reset gate X and all downstream gates to pending while preserving existing tasks and completed work. Each re-entry must append an audit entry describing the reopened gate, reason, optional scope delta, actor, timestamp, and gates reset.

scenarios.title: Cascade from discovery resets downstream gates
given: A change has completed proposal, discovery, design, planning, execution, acceptance, and release is still pending
then: discovery, design, planning, execution, acceptance, and release are reset to pending
proposal remains satisfied

scenarios.title: Tasks remain intact after cascade reset
given: A change has existing tasks, including completed tasks
then: All existing tasks remain on the change
Task status and task content are preserved

scenarios.title: Re-entry appends audit history
given: A change has been reopened via adv_change_reenter
then: A reentry_history entry is appended with from_gate, reason, reopened_by, approval_evidence, reopened_at, and gates_reset
scope_delta is included when provided

requirements.title: Seven-Gate Collaborative Workflow
requirements.body: The canonical ADV workflow is seven sequential gates: proposal, discovery, design, planning, execution, acceptance, release. Gates must be completed in order. A change cannot be archived until all seven gates are satisfied (status 'done' or 'skipped').

scenarios.title: Sequential gate enforcement
given: A change with the proposal gate pending
scenarios.when: adv_gate_complete is called for the discovery gate
then: The call is rejected
The response identifies proposal as the blocking gate

scenarios.title: Archive requires all seven gates satisfied
given: A change with gates proposal through acceptance marked done
The release gate still pending
then: The archive is rejected with incomplete-gates error
release is listed as the remaining gate

requirements.title: Archive Finalization Refreshes Basis and Preserves Cleanup Safety
requirements.body: Phase 9 Git Finalization must refresh the current default-branch basis before deciding local merge-back versus PR workflow. Clean low-risk cases prefer a linear-history fast path (`--ff-only` when already current, reconcile only when needed). Conflicting or risky cases must stop or route to PR workflow before cleanup. Worktree deletion remains forbidden until merged-state verification proves the change branch is fully integrated.

scenarios.title: Clean archive refresh uses local fast path
given: A change branch is already on the current default-branch basis
No overlap-risk or PR-only policy applies
scenarios.when: Phase 9 Git Finalization chooses an integration path
then: The archive uses the local `--ff-only` path
No branch rewrite is required

scenarios.title: Conflicting reconcile stops before cleanup
given: A change branch must reconcile with a fresher default branch
Compatibility preflight or rebase finds conflicts
scenarios.when: Phase 9 Git Finalization evaluates the reconcile path
then: The archive reports the conflicting files
The archive does not delete the worktree

scenarios.title: Risky archive routes to PR workflow
given: A change has overlap-risk, PR-only policy, or non-fast-forward publish risk
scenarios.when: Phase 9 Git Finalization chooses an integration path
then: The archive routes to PR workflow instead of forcing local merge-back
Cleanup remains blocked until merged-state verification succeeds

requirements.title: Human Checkpoint Contract
requirements.body: ADV must pause for human input only at explicit approval/judgment checkpoints and auto-continue through clean agent-owned workflow steps. Human checkpoints are: proposal confirmation, agreement sign-off, design approval when real tradeoffs depend on user values, when the design validator returns CONFLICT, or when contract-compromise risk is present, acceptance, archive sign-off, cancellation approval, and doom-loop recovery. All other clean workflow steps (discovery, deterministic design, prep, apply, harden, and scope-driven re-entry) proceed sequentially without prompting the user when no unresolved user-value tradeoff, contract-compromise risk, or required approval exists.

scenarios.title: Clean agent-owned step auto-continues
given: A change has completed the proposal gate
The next step is discovery with no unresolved user-value tradeoffs
scenarios.when: The ADV orchestrator evaluates the next gate
then: Discovery proceeds without prompting the user
No question tool call is made for the gate transition

scenarios.title: Human checkpoint pauses for approval
given: A change has completed the acceptance gate via /adv-review
Archive sign-off is the next step
scenarios.when: The ADV orchestrator evaluates the next gate
then: The orchestrator stops and presents a change report
The user is asked for explicit sign-off via inline handoff text per docs/command-voice-standard.md § Inline Approval Voice (Tier B)

scenarios.title: Design approval conditional on tradeoffs
given: A change has a straightforward design with no user-value tradeoffs
then: The orchestrator proceeds to planning without a design-approval pause

scenarios.title: Apply auto-continues across task boundaries
given: A change is in the execution gate with multiple pending ready tasks
No enumerated human checkpoint has triggered (no doom-loop, no environmental blocker, no cancellation, no re-entry, no unresolved judgment call)
scenarios.when: A task completes successfully and `adv_task_ready` returns another pending task
then: `/adv-apply` proceeds immediately to the next task's TDD loop
No "task complete", "section complete", "progress update", or "shall I continue?" pause is emitted
No question tool call is made between tasks

scenarios.title: Apply forbids execution-start approval pause
given: A change has completed planning and is entering the execution gate
Judgment-call surfacing (Phase 1.5) has already resolved any pending user input
then: No "Begin work / Modify criteria / Cancel" prompt or equivalent execution-start approval is emitted
The first ready task's TDD phase starts directly

scenarios.title: Contract-compromise risk triggers design pause
given: A change is at the design gate
The agent identifies that delivering the design would require compromising agreed acceptance criteria, explicit constraints, or stated avoidances
scenarios.when: The orchestrator evaluates whether to proceed from design to planning
then: The orchestrator pauses for human input before proceeding
The design approval checkpoint is triggered regardless of whether user-value tradeoffs exist

requirements.title: Validated In-Scope Findings Resolved In-Change
requirements.body: When /adv-review or /adv-harden validates an actionable finding or suggestion as in-scope, the current change must fix it before completion. No report-only, future-work, or accepted-debt path is permitted for validated in-scope findings. Findings may only be left unresolved if rejected with evidence showing they are invalid or out of scope.

scenarios.title: Validated suggestion implemented before completion
given: /adv-review validates a suggestion as in-scope and correct
then: The validated suggestion is implemented and verified in the current change
The finding status is updated to fixed

scenarios.title: Report-only path rejected for in-scope findings
given: /adv-harden identifies an actionable in-scope finding
then: No report-only or future-work option is offered
The finding must be fixed before the release gate can complete

scenarios.title: Rejection with evidence is permitted
given: /adv-review flags a suggestion as potentially in-scope
scenarios.when: Investigation determines the suggestion is invalid or out of scope
then: The finding is rejected with documented evidence
The rejection does not block gate completion

requirements.title: Touched-Scope Quality Ownership
requirements.body: A change owns quality and test coverage for: (1) directly touched implementation files, (2) adjacent tests and docs needed for correctness, and (3) same-pattern quality or test issues in the local touched subsystem. This ownership boundary must remain local enough to avoid implicit repo-wide refactors. /adv-prep must synthesize tasks covering touched-scope obligations, /adv-apply must verify them before execution completes, and /adv-review and /adv-harden must enforce them.

scenarios.title: Adjacent test gaps addressed
given: A change modifies an implementation file
The corresponding test file has gaps in coverage for the touched code
then: The test gaps are addressed as part of the change
The execution gate is not marked complete while known test gaps remain in touched files

scenarios.title: Same-pattern issues fixed in local subsystem
given: A change fixes a defect pattern in one file
The same pattern exists in other files within the local touched subsystem
scenarios.when: Review or harden identifies the related instances
then: The same-pattern instances are fixed in the current change
The fixes are verified before gate completion

scenarios.title: Ownership boundary remains local
given: A change touches files in one subsystem
A similar pattern exists in unrelated subsystems
then: Only the local touched subsystem is in scope
Unrelated subsystems are not implicitly pulled into the change

requirements.title: Design Stage Requires Independent Validation
requirements.body: Before the design gate can complete, /adv-design must run an independent validation pass via an independent, read-only, externally informed validator sub-agent. The validator must be a distinct agent from the designer (different model or isolated context), have read-only access to ADV state, and possess external research capabilities (documentation lookup, web search). The validator assesses correctness, simplicity, spec-law compliance, and key alternatives. The current implementation of this capability is adv-researcher. Validator failure or timeout results in an INCONCLUSIVE warning and does not block gate completion.

scenarios.title: Validator runs before design gate completion
given: A change has a confirmed agreement and completed design work
then: An independent validation sub-agent pass runs before adv_gate_complete is called for the design gate
The validator is a distinct agent from the designer with read-only state access and external research capabilities
The validator assesses at least: correctness, simplicity, spec-law compliance, and key alternatives

scenarios.title: Validator failure results in INCONCLUSIVE, not a block
given: The validator sub-agent fails, returns empty, or times out
then: The result is recorded as INCONCLUSIVE with a warning
The design gate is not blocked by the validator failure
The warning is surfaced in the /adv-design presentation output

requirements.title: Validation Findings Included in Design Presentation
requirements.body: When /adv-design summarizes the design, it must include the validator verdict and findings from the design validation step. VALIDATED shows a brief clean-pass note. CAUTION shows findings inline. CONFLICT shows conflict details. INCONCLUSIVE shows a warning. Legacy designs without validation data omit the section silently.

scenarios.title: Clean-pass note shown for VALIDATED verdict
given: The design validator returned VALIDATED
then: The output includes a one-line clean-pass note (e.g. 'Validator: clean pass')
No detailed findings are shown

scenarios.title: Conflict details shown for CONFLICT verdict
given: The design validator returned CONFLICT with findings
then: The conflict details and unresolved findings are shown to the user
The presentation pauses for user resolution before proceeding to planning

requirements.title: Critical Validator Disagreement Requires Explicit Handling
requirements.body: When the design validator returns a CONFLICT verdict, the orchestrator must not silently auto-continue to planning. The conflict must be surfaced to the user or resolved inline before /adv-prep can proceed.

scenarios.title: CONFLICT verdict blocks silent auto-continue to planning
given: The design validator returned a CONFLICT verdict with unresolved findings
scenarios.when: The orchestrator evaluates whether to proceed from design to planning
then: The orchestrator does not silently proceed to /adv-prep
The conflict is surfaced to the user via /adv-design presentation pause or inline resolution attempt

scenarios.title: VALIDATED and CAUTION verdicts auto-continue
given: The design validator returned VALIDATED or CAUTION
scenarios.when: The orchestrator evaluates whether to proceed from design to planning
then: Planning proceeds without a new user-facing checkpoint (assuming no other user-value tradeoffs)

requirements.title: Contract-Compromise Risk Requires Design Pause
requirements.body: When an agent identifies that a proposed design can only be delivered by compromising agreed acceptance criteria, explicit constraints, or stated avoidances, the orchestrator must pause for human input before proceeding to planning. This check is independent of the design validator verdict and must trigger even when the validator returns VALIDATED or CAUTION.

scenarios.title: Contract-compromise risk triggers design pause
given: A design is being evaluated
The agent determines that implementing the design would violate an agreed acceptance criterion or explicit constraint
scenarios.when: The orchestrator evaluates whether to proceed from design to planning
then: The orchestrator pauses for human input
No silent auto-continue to planning occurs

scenarios.title: No compromise risk auto-continues
given: A design is being evaluated
The agent confirms the design can be delivered without compromising any acceptance criteria, constraints, or stated avoidances
scenarios.when: The orchestrator evaluates whether to proceed from design to planning
then: Planning proceeds without a new design-approval checkpoint (assuming no other user-value tradeoffs)

requirements.title: Canonical Apply Tool Path for Inline TDD
requirements.body: For ordinary inline TDD work, /adv-apply MUST explicitly name editing tools (edit, write, morph_edit where appropriate) as the correct path for creating or modifying test files, and MUST explicitly name adv_run_test as the primary red/green test execution path.

scenarios.title: Red and green contract lines name adv_run_test
given: The /adv-apply command contract is present
scenarios.when: Its Red and Green phase guidance is inspected
then: The Red phase line names adv_run_test with phase 'red'
The Green phase line names adv_run_test with phase 'green'

scenarios.title: Apply contract names editing tools for test-file changes
given: The /adv-apply command contract is present
scenarios.when: Its ordinary inline-TDD file-editing guidance is inspected
then: At least one supported editing tool is named for test-file creation or modification
Raw shell file-writing is not presented as the normal path

scenarios.title: Fallback framing keeps adv_task_evidence secondary
given: The /adv-apply command or adjacent remediation guidance references adv_task_evidence
then: adv_task_evidence is described as fallback or externally captured evidence path
It is not described as the primary inline-TDD execution path

requirements.title: Apply Contract Regression Anchors
requirements.body: The repository MUST maintain asset or regression tests that fail when canonical inline-TDD path wording is removed from /adv-apply or when shell-authored test-file content is reintroduced as normal-path guidance.

scenarios.title: Asset tests guard canonical path wording
given: The /adv-apply contract is covered by asset tests
scenarios.when: Canonical Red/Green adv_run_test wording is removed
then: The asset or regression suite fails

scenarios.title: Asset tests guard anti-pattern row
given: The /adv-apply contract is covered by asset tests
scenarios.when: The shell-authored test-file anti-pattern row is removed or weakened
then: The asset or regression suite fails

requirements.title: Runtime Enforcement for Inline-TDD Bash Workarounds
requirements.body: During active inline-TDD work, runtime enforcement MUST treat shell-authored test-file content and direct test-runner bash differently. Shell-authored test-file content targeting test-glob paths is prohibited and MUST be blocked. Direct test-runner bash without a matching recent adv_run_test signal MUST emit an advisory but MUST NOT be blocked. These are firm defaults, not feature-flagged rollout modes.

scenarios.title: Shell-authored test-file content is blocked
given: A change is active
An inline-TDD task is in progress
A bash command writes file content to a test-glob path
scenarios.when: The bash command is checked by runtime enforcement
then: The command is blocked
The error tells the agent to use editing tools and adv_run_test instead

scenarios.title: Direct test-runner bash is advisory only
given: A change is active
An inline-TDD task is in progress
A bash command runs a test runner without a matching recent adv_run_test signal
scenarios.when: The bash command is checked by runtime enforcement
then: The command is not blocked
An advisory is emitted directing the agent to prefer adv_run_test

requirements.title: Gate Handoff Voice Spine
requirements.body: Every /adv-* command that emits a user-facing gate-transition message MUST use the Gate Handoff Voice spine: Problem / Chosen direction / Delivered, followed by a blockquote wayfinder block. The blockquote MUST contain three rows: bolded `**{change-id}**`, the gate transition `{gate} ✓ → {next-gate}`, and an arrow-prefixed runnable command `→ `/adv-{next-command} {change-id}``. The command shown MUST be the single command needed to continue — no redundant or alternative command lines. Canonical source: docs/command-voice-standard.md § Gate Handoff Voice.

scenarios.title: Handoff follows spine with blockquote wayfinder block
given: An /adv-* command completes a gate and emits a user-facing gate-transition message
then: All three narrative spine headings are present: Problem, Chosen direction, Delivered, followed by a blockquote wayfinder block below a --- separator
The blockquote contains a row with `**{change-id}**` (bolded change ID)
The blockquote contains a row with `{gate} ✓ → {next-gate}` (gate transition)
The blockquote contains an arrow-prefixed row `→ `/adv-{next-command} {change-id}`` showing exactly one runnable command

The archive terminal variant uses a single-line blockquote `> **{change-id}** · release ✓ ·` followed by a terminal verb (Shipped. when push succeeds and assets propagate to the global install, Merged locally. when no remote is configured or push is skipped or push fails) instead of the standard three-row wayfinder block

When the handoff is paired with a human-checkpoint approval, reply instructions appear as plain prose below the blockquote (not inside it); the three-section spine (Problem / Chosen direction / Delivered) is unchanged

scenarios.title: No mechanics leakage
given: An /adv-* command completes a gate and emits a user-facing gate-transition message
then: No todo checklists appear as primary handoff content
No step-completed logs appear as primary handoff content
No orchestration summaries appear as primary handoff content
No sub-agent bookkeeping appears as primary handoff content
No gate checkbox banners appear as primary handoff content

scenarios.title: Auto-continue transitions unaffected
given: rq-autonomy01 permits auto-continue between stages
No unresolved user-value tradeoff exists
No required approval is pending
scenarios.when: The agent proceeds without emitting a user-facing message
then: No handoff message is emitted
No handoff validation is required for the silent transition

scenarios.title: Blockquote wayfinder block replaces Next sections
given: An /adv-* command completes a gate and emits a user-facing gate-transition message
then: ## Next stage and ## Next headings are absent from the handoff
A blockquote wayfinder block appears after ## Delivered with three rows: change-id, gate transition, arrow-prefixed runnable command

The archive terminal variant ends with a single-line blockquote `> **{change-id}** · release ✓ ·` followed by a terminal verb (Shipped. or Merged locally. depending on push state) and no separate labeled block
Optional reply instructions for human checkpoints (Inline Approval Voice) appear as plain prose below the blockquote, not inside it

scenarios.title: Blockquote wayfinder shows only the needed command
given: An /adv-* command completes a gate and emits a user-facing gate-transition message
scenarios.when: The blockquote wayfinder block is inspected
then: Exactly one runnable command is shown in the wayfinder block (in the arrow-prefixed row)
No redundant alternative command lines appear
The command shown is the single next action needed to continue

requirements.title: Filter-Aware Bulk Close
requirements.body: adv_change_bulk_close must support closing multiple changes in a single approved tool call, using either an explicit ID list or filter-based selection, with fail-all semantics on invalid targets and a structured result envelope.

scenarios.title: Explicit ID list close
given: A list of valid active change IDs
scenarios.when: adv_change_bulk_close is called with kind: 'explicit' and those IDs
then: All specified changes are closed with the provided reason
The result envelope lists each change with success or error status
If reason: 'superseded', at most one survivor change ID may be provided

scenarios.title: Filter-based close requires explicit filter
given: A repository with changes in various states
then: The call must supply either a status filter (draft or pending) OR a staleness filter (createdBefore or lastActivityBefore)
No implicit default status is applied

scenarios.title: Fail-all on protected targets
given: A bulk close request targeting a mix of valid and invalid changes
scenarios.when: Any resolved target is active, archived, closed, nonexistent, ambiguous, or duplicated
then: The entire request fails before any mutation
The error identifies every invalid target and its specific failure reason

scenarios.title: Empty match is a structured error
given: A filter-based bulk close that matches zero changes
then: A structured error is returned with success: false
The message clearly states that no changes matched the filter
No silent no-op occurs

scenarios.title: Result envelope mirrors task-cancel pattern
given: A bulk close request that partially or fully succeeds
then: The envelope contains success, closed (count), results (array of per-change entries), and message
Each per-change entry includes changeId, success, and optional error

scenarios.title: No hard delete
given: Any bulk close call
then: Changes are closed (status moved to closed) but never purged or hard-deleted
Audit metadata is preserved for every closed change

scenarios.title: Existing close signature unchanged
given: The existing adv_change_close tool
then: Its signature and behavior remain identical to before adv_change_bulk_close was added

requirements.title: Source-Appropriate Due Diligence for Unknown Capability Questions
requirements.body: ADV-managed guidance (orchestrator agent text, synced overlays, and accompanying drift tests) must require source-appropriate due diligence before answering, recommending, or deciding on unknown platform, architecture, or capability questions. Diligence may use any appropriate mix of evidence sources (local code via lgrep/read, repo history or repo examples, GitHub examples, official docs, web research, or similar); the evidence bar is not a fixed source stack. Requests like "quick answer", "from your knowledge", or "don't research" may change response brevity only and must not lower the evidence bar. If required diligence cannot be completed, the response must stop and surface the blockage instead of presenting an unverified recommendation as settled.

scenarios.title: Unknown capability question triggers source-appropriate diligence
given: An unknown platform, architecture, or capability question is posed to an ADV-managed agent
scenarios.when: The agent prepares an answer, recommendation, or decision
then: The agent gathers source-appropriate evidence before answering
Evidence may come from local code inspection, repo history or repo examples, GitHub examples, official docs, web research, or other relevant sources chosen to fit the question
No carve-out permits skipping diligence on the basis that the question is local, short, or familiar

scenarios.title: Quick-answer requests change brevity only
given: The user requests a "quick answer", asks "from your knowledge", or says "don't research"
The question still requires due diligence under rq-dueDiligence01.1
then: The response may be shortened or compressed
The evidence bar is not lowered; diligence is still performed before recommending or deciding

scenarios.title: Blocked diligence stops and surfaces the blockage
given: Required diligence cannot be completed (for example: docs, research tools, or evidence sources are unavailable)
scenarios.when: The agent would otherwise present a directional answer or recommendation
then: The agent stops instead of presenting an unverified direction as settled
The response surfaces the specific blockage or missing evidence
No carve-out permits proceeding with an unverified recommendation

scenarios.title: Guidance surfaces and drift tests encode the rule
given: The repo contains ADV orchestrator agent text, synced overlays, and routing asset tests
then: The ADV agent and plan agent sources describe due-diligence-first routing for unknown capability questions
The synced overlays (adv.overlay.md, plan.overlay.md) carry the same rule
Regression tests fail if the legacy carve-out-first wording returns

requirements.title: Durable Task-Run Lifecycle Ledger
requirements.body: /adv-apply task execution must maintain a Temporal-owned task-run ledger that records lifecycle phase, required next action, resume hint, evidence, verification, checkpoint, and blocker/failure events without moving OpenCode tool/model/file-edit execution into Temporal activities. The ledger must preserve existing inline TDD, checkpoint-before-done, and no-pause apply-loop semantics.

scenarios.title: Task-run status exposes safe resume point
given: An /adv-apply task has started or partially completed
scenarios.when: adv_task_run_status is called for that task
then: The response includes the current phase
The response includes requiredNextAction and resumeHint
The response summarizes baseline, evidence, verification, checkpoint, attempts, and recent events when present

scenarios.title: Evidence and checkpoint events are linked to the ledger
given: An /adv-apply task records red evidence, green evidence, verification, or checkpoint result
then: A task-run ledger event is recorded with a deterministic idempotency key
Duplicate idempotency keys do not append duplicate events or re-advance phase
The existing tdd_evidence and checkpoint outputs remain intact

scenarios.title: Ledger survives Temporal continue-as-new
given: A change workflow has task_runs state
scenarios.when: The workflow continues as new to bound history size
then: The task_runs state is included in the seed state
Handler-level idempotency data survives workflow-run rollover
No task-run phase or resume information is silently reset

scenarios.title: Temporal remains the runtime state authority
given: Task-run lifecycle state is persisted
scenarios.when: The runtime reads or mutates task-run state
then: The authoritative runtime state lives in Temporal workflow state
Disk artifacts may support recovery or tests but are not the task-run runtime source of truth
No SQLite or replacement local database is introduced for task-run state

scenarios.title: No direct Temporal execution of OpenCode side effects in v1
given: A task-run event represents evidence, verification, or checkpoint outcome
then: OpenCode agents still perform file edits, shell/test commands, model calls, sub-agent calls, and git commits
Temporal records externally supplied results rather than directly executing those side effects

scenarios.title: Apply-loop autonomy is preserved
given: A task-run ledger indicates the next resume action
then: The ledger guides the agent to the next apply-loop step
No new user pause or approval checkpoint is introduced
Existing blocker, doom-loop, cancellation, re-entry, and acceptance checkpoints remain unchanged

requirements.title: Inline Approval at Named Human Checkpoints
requirements.body: ADV's seven named human checkpoints (proposal confirmation, agreement sign-off, design approval, prep approval, acceptance, archive sign-off, cancellation approval) MUST use inline handoff text — composed with the Gate Handoff Voice spine — instead of the question tool. The inline pattern MUST emit reply instructions covering approve, redirect via slash command, revise, and stop. Reply parsing tiers MUST be: Tier A (reversible — proposal/agreement/design/prep/acceptance) uses whitelist + LLM fallback for natural-language replies; Tier B (irreversible — archive sign-off, cancellation) uses whitelist-only with no LLM fallback. Non-checkpoint question tool uses (change-id selection, doom-loop recovery, drift detection, AC clarification rounds, investment check-in, judgment calls, triage commands) remain unaffected. Canonical source: docs/command-voice-standard.md § Inline Approval Voice.

scenarios.title: Checkpoint approval uses inline handoff
given: An ADV workflow reaches a named human checkpoint
scenarios.when: The agent presents the checkpoint to the user
then: The presentation uses the Gate Handoff Voice spine plus inline reply instructions
The question tool is not used for the checkpoint approval

scenarios.title: Tier A whitelist + LLM fallback for reversible checkpoints
given: A reversible checkpoint (proposal, agreement, design, prep, acceptance) is presented
User replies with a whitelist word OR an ambiguous reply
then: Whitelist words (continue, go, approve, yes, ok, proceed, accept, lgtm, etc.) trigger immediate approval
Ambiguous replies are classified by LLM into approve / revise / redirect / stop / unclear
Unclear replies trigger re-prompt

scenarios.title: Tier B whitelist-only for irreversible actions
given: An irreversible checkpoint (archive sign-off, cancellation approval) is presented
then: Only exact whitelist matches trigger approval
LLM fallback is not used
Anything else triggers re-prompt with the same options
Archive sign-off executes in the same response as the whitelist-match acknowledgment with no separate confirmation-echo turn

scenarios.title: Cancellation uses structured inline format
given: A cancellation approval is needed
then: The agent emits a numbered per-task list as inline prose
Reply instructions cover approve all, reject all, keep N, cancel N, stop
Replies are parsed by exact regex; LLM fallback is not used
Ambiguous replies trigger re-prompt

scenarios.title: Prep gate machine contract preserved
given: The user replies with a Tier A whitelist word at the prep checkpoint
scenarios.when: The agent calls adv_gate_complete gateId: planning
then: userApproved: true is passed
The machine contract is satisfied
Inline approval is the upstream signal source independent of the API surface

scenarios.title: Non-checkpoint question uses unaffected
given: A non-checkpoint workflow step uses the question tool (change-id selection, doom-loop, drift detection, AC clarification round, investment check-in, judgment call, triage)
then: The question tool continues to be used
The inline approval pattern does not apply

scenarios.title: Exact shown Tier A continuation command counts as approval
given: A Tier A checkpoint (proposal, agreement, design, prep, acceptance) is presented with a blockquote wayfinder block showing a specific continuation command (e.g., `/adv-apply {change-id}`)
The user invokes that exact command while the checkpoint is pending
scenarios.when: The agent processes the command invocation
then: The invocation counts as explicit approval equivalent to a Tier A whitelist word
The agent completes the pending gate with userApproved: true
The agent proceeds immediately to the next stage without a second approval prompt

scenarios.title: Tier B remains whitelist-only with no command-as-approval bypass
given: A Tier B checkpoint (archive sign-off, cancellation approval) is presented
scenarios.when: The user invokes a slash command or provides a non-whitelist reply
then: Only exact whitelist matches trigger approval
No slash command invocation counts as approval
LLM fallback is not used
Anything else triggers re-prompt with the same options

requirements.title: Fast-Follow Schema Contract
requirements.body: The ChangeSchema must support an optional `fast_follow_of` field that records same-project parent lineage. The `adv_change_create` tool must accept `parent_change_id` and enforce mutual exclusion with `target_path`.

scenarios.title: parent_change_id creates fast_follow_of metadata
given: A valid parent change ID in the current project
then: The new change has fast_follow_of: { parent_change_id, linked_at } set
linked_at is an ISO8601 timestamp

scenarios.title: Backward compatibility without fast_follow_of
given: A change created without parent_change_id
then: The fast_follow_of field is absent
Parsing succeeds normally

scenarios.title: Mutual exclusion with target_path
given: Both target_path and parent_change_id are provided
then: A mutual-exclusion error is returned
No change is created

requirements.title: Inline-Approval Protocol for Non-Campsite Scope Discovery
requirements.body: When non-P23-campsite-eligible scope is discovered during /adv-apply, /adv-review, or /adv-harden, the agent must emit a Tier A inline prompt with options reenter/split/keep/cancel. The agent must never silently absorb discovered scope.

scenarios.title: Non-campsite scope triggers inline prompt
given: Non-P23-campsite-eligible scope discovered during /adv-apply, /adv-review, or /adv-harden
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates the discovered scope
then: A Tier A inline prompt is emitted with options: reenter {gate}, split, keep, cancel
The agent never silently absorbs the scope

scenarios.title: Split creates fast-follow child
given: User replies split to the scope-discovery prompt
then: adv_change_create is called with parent_change_id set to the current change
The new change is a fast-follow child

scenarios.title: Keep with new objectives requires re-entry
given: User replies keep and the absorbed scope adds new objectives or acceptance criteria
then: adv_change_reenter is invoked per rq-scopeReentry01
Keep does not bypass re-entry when scope adds objectives/AC

scenarios.title: Campsite-eligible scope applied freely
given: P23-campsite-eligible adjacent scope (any size, clear, safe, focused)
then: The campsite-rule is applied freely without prompting
No inline approval is required

requirements.title: Lineage Display in List, Show, and Status
requirements.body: Tools that surface change data must display fast-follow lineage: adv_change_show includes _fastFollowOrigin, adv_change_list annotates entries with parent_change_id, and adv_status prefixes child labels and references parents in recommendations.

scenarios.title: adv_change_show surfaces _fastFollowOrigin
given: A change with fast_follow_of set
then: Output includes _fastFollowOrigin parallel to _crossProjectOrigin
_fastFollowOrigin contains note, parent_change_id, and linked_at

scenarios.title: adv_change_list annotates parent_change_id
given: Changes with fast_follow_of in the list
then: Entries with fast_follow_of include parent_change_id at the top level
Children remain top-level (not nested)

scenarios.title: adv_status prefixes and references parents
given: Changes with fast_follow_of in the project
then: Child change labels are prefixed with ↳
Recommendations reference the parent change ID
Archived parents are annotated with (archived)

requirements.title: Size Alone Is Not Grounds for Split-Suggestion
requirements.body: Once a change has completed the prep gate with userApproved, the agent must not suggest splitting based on size, task count, or complexity alone. Size-triggered concerns route through cost-governance Phase 1.5 judgment-call surfacing only.

scenarios.title: No split-suggestion after prep approval
given: A change has completed the prep gate with userApproved
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates whether to suggest splitting
then: The agent does not emit split-suggestions based on size, task count, or complexity alone
Execution proceeds as planned

scenarios.title: Size concerns route through cost-governance
given: Size-triggered concerns exist during execution
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates how to surface concerns
then: Concerns are routed through cost-governance Phase 1.5 judgment-call surfacing
No split-suggestion is made

scenarios.title: Hardstop remains advisory
given: Cost-governance hardstop tier fires
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates the hardstop signal
then: The hardstop is advisory only
It does not auto-trigger split or adv_change_reenter
